content.body:
**Source:** PR #7 (Fleet Manager Evaluation for DK8S RP)

## Context
The Fleet Manager evaluation concluded with a DEFER recommendation. The primary blocker is Workload Identity / FIC automation gaps. This must be resolved before Fleet Manager adoption can be reconsidered.

## Research Tasks

1. **FIC Creation Security Gap Analysis**
   - OIDC issuer and service account name are mutable 螕脟枚 creates security risk
   - Chained FIC (Workload -> MI -> AAD App) blocked by Entra limitations
   - Document current state and potential mitigations

2. **AAD Pod Identity -> Workload Identity Migration Status**
   - Track current migration progress across DK8S clusters
   - Identify remaining blockers

3. **Fleet Manager vs ArgoCD Swim Lane Definition**
   - Define clear separation: Fleet Manager (cluster lifecycle) vs ArgoCD (app delivery)
   - Document decision criteria for when to revisit Fleet Manager adoption

4. **Monitor Adoption Triggers**
   - DK8S grows beyond 50 clusters
   - Production incident requiring cluster replacement (not in-place upgrade)
   - AKFM ships native ArgoCD integration

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] FIC security gaps documented with mitigations
- [ ] Migration status tracked
- [ ] Swim lane document created
- [ ] Adoption trigger monitoring plan defined

**Effort:** Medium | **Priority:** Medium 螕脟枚 strategic prerequisite
content.title: Research: Workload Identity / FIC Automation 螕脟枚 Fleet Manager Prerequisite

content.body:
**Source:** DK8S Runtime Platform Teams channel (last 48 hours)
**Reported by:** Nada Jasikova, Karan (via WorkIQ detection)
**Detected:** 2026-03-07T17:45Z

## Active Incident
STG-EUS2-28 cluster is experiencing cascading failures:
- **Draino:** Nodes repeatedly attempting drain, failing
- **Karpenter:** >20%% unhealthy nodes reported
- **Istio ztunnel:** Pods failing to start despite nodes being Ready
- **NodeStuck automation:** Potentially too aggressive 螕脟枚 deleting nodes when Istio daemonsets are unhealthy for non-node reasons

## Correlation with Squad Stability Work
This incident directly validates the mitigations planned in:
- Issue #24 (Tier 1): I1 螕脟枚 Establish Istio Exclusion List for Infrastructure Components
- Issue #25 (Tier 2): I2 螕脟枚 ztunnel Health Monitoring with Automatic Rollback
- Issue #25 (Tier 2): N1 螕脟枚 Zone-Aware NAT Gateway Monitoring

The cascading pattern (Draino to Karpenter to Istio ztunnel to node deletion) is exactly the failure mode B-Elanna Tier 1/2 plan addresses.

## Also from Teams
- FedRAMP P0: nginx-ingress-heartbeat vulnerabilities on gov clusters 螕脟枚 decision needed
- NodeStuck automation: Karan proposed excluding Istio daemonsets from deletion automation
- PIM/Kusto MI: Onboarding process gaps need protocol definition

## Action Items
- [ ] Share this correlation with DK8S platform leads to accelerate Tier 1 execution
- [ ] Consider fast-tracking I1 (Istio exclusion list) given active incident
- [ ] Review Karan proposal to exclude Istio from node deletion automation

---
Bridged from Teams via WorkIQ by teams-monitor skill
Detected: 2026-03-07T17:45Z

content.body:
**Source:** PR #8 (DK8S Stability & Configuration Management Analysis)

## Context
Tier 2 improvements are high impact, medium effort. Should be planned for next sprint/quarter.

## Tier 2 Improvements (DO SOON)

1. **N1: Zone-Aware NAT Gateway Monitoring** 螕脟枚 Differentiate AZ-scoped vs region-wide failures, reduce false Sev2 pages
2. **N2: DNS Health Checks in Node Readiness Gates** 螕脟枚 Prevent pod scheduling on DNS-broken nodes
3. **C2: Deployment Feedback Webhook** 螕脟枚 Report EV2 step failures back to IDP for visibility
4. **I2: ztunnel Health Monitoring with Automatic Rollback** 螕脟枚 Add rollback trigger if ztunnel failure rate exceeds threshold
5. **C3: Create Breaking Change Changelog per ConfigGen Release** 螕脟枚 Include migration guides for each breaking change

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Each improvement scoped with effort estimate
- [ ] Implementation plan created
- [ ] Prioritized for upcoming sprint planning

**Effort:** Medium per item | **Priority:** High
content.title: DK8S Stability: Plan Tier 2 High-Impact Improvements

items.title: DK8S Stability: Plan Tier 2 High-Impact Improvements

content.body:
**Source:** PR #8 (DK8S Stability & Configuration Management Analysis)

## Context
Tier 3 initiatives are strategic, high-effort improvements that should be planned for next quarter. These address fundamental architectural limitations.

## Strategic Initiatives

### Configuration Management
1. **L7: Decentralize ConfigGen Ownership** 螕脟枚 Move from monolithic central repo to per-team config repos with shared schema validation
2. **L8: CRD-First Configuration Model** 螕脟枚 Replace file-based config expansion with Kubernetes CRDs managed by operators (3-12 months)
3. **L13: Additional STG Ring for Sovereign Clouds** 螕脟枚 Pre-prod validation ring for US sovereign clouds

### Networking Resilience
4. **L1: Multi-AZ NAT Gateway Failover** 螕脟枚 Redundant NAT Gateways per AZ with automatic failover
5. **L2: DNS Caching and Fallback** 螕脟枚 Local DNS cache per node with regional resolver fallback
6. **L3: Ingress Migration to AKS App Routing** 螕脟枚 Complete NGINX deprecation with zero-downtime

### Service Mesh Maturity
7. **L4: Istio Ambient Mode Graduated Rollout** 螕脟枚 Ring-based mesh enrollment with auto-canary
8. **L5: Mesh Observability Integration** 螕脟枚 Istio telemetry integrated with Geneva
9. **L6: Permanent Infrastructure Component Isolation** 螕脟枚 Exclude platform daemonsets from mesh permanently

### Platform Resilience
10. **L11: Self-Hosted VPA at Scale** 螕脟枚 Replace AKS VPA add-on for >1k pod clusters
11. **L12: Minimum D8 Node Size Standard** 螕脟枚 Enforce D8+ to prevent daemonset overhead starvation
12. **L14: RP-Driven Deployment Model** 螕脟枚 Resource Provider as control plane (3-12 months)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Each initiative scoped with effort/impact assessment
- [ ] Prioritized for quarterly planning
- [ ] Dependencies mapped (e.g., L8 depends on RP registration progress)

**Effort:** High per initiative | **Priority:** Strategic 螕脟枚 next quarter planning
content.title: DK8S Stability: Tier 3 Strategic Architecture Initiatives

items.title: DK8S Stability: Tier 3 Strategic Architecture Initiatives

content.body:
## Problem

All GitHub Actions workflows are failing with **0 steps executed** and completing in ~3 seconds. This affects:

- **Squad Issue Notification** workflow (3 consecutive failures)
- **FedRAMP Validation** workflow (5+ consecutive failures)
- **Helm/Kustomize Drift Detection** workflow

## Diagnosis

Jobs start but no steps execute, suggesting the runner cannot be provisioned. Possible causes:
1. **GitHub Actions minutes exhausted** (private repo billing)
2. **Repository Actions permissions** restricted
3. **Runner availability** issue

## Recent Runs (all failing)

| Run ID | Workflow | Time | Steps |
|--------|----------|------|-------|
| 22815633820 | Squad Issue Notification | 2026-03-08 06:30 | 0 |
| 22815003061 | Squad Issue Notification | 2026-03-08 05:46 | 0 |
| 22815003043 | FedRAMP Validation | 2026-03-08 05:46 | 0 |
| 22814962234 | FedRAMP Validation | 2026-03-08 05:43 | 0 |
| 22814900671 | Squad Issue Notification | 2026-03-08 05:39 | 0 |

## Action Required

1. Check **Settings 螕氓脝 Actions 螕氓脝 General** for the repo 螕脟枚 ensure Actions are enabled
2. Check **Settings 螕氓脝 Billing 螕氓脝 Actions** 螕脟枚 verify minutes are available
3. If private repo, consider making it internal or upgrading plan
4. Re-run a workflow after fixing to verify

/cc @tamirdresher_microsoft

content.body:
# FedRAMP Dashboard Cache Configuration Review - April 2026

**Review Date:** Tuesday, April 1, 2026 at 10:00 AM PT  
**Review Period:** March 8, 2026 to April 7, 2026 (30 days)  
**Attendees:** Data (Code Expert), Infrastructure Lead, SRE On-Call

**Note:** This is the FIRST cache review following deployment of monitoring in PR #108.

---

## Pre-Review Checklist

- [ ] Query Application Insights for 30-day metrics
- [ ] Prepare access pattern analysis
- [ ] Review any open action items from previous month
- [ ] Check for cache-related incidents or alerts

---

## Meeting Agenda

1. **Performance Metrics** (10 min)
   - Cache hit rate: Current vs. SLO (螕毛脩70%)
   - Backend query reduction: Current vs. target (80-85%)
   - P95 latency: Current vs. target (<500ms)

2. **Access Pattern Analysis** (10 min)
   - Top query combinations
   - Traffic patterns (peak/off-peak/weekend)
   - Unique parameter combinations

3. **Incidents & Anomalies** (5 min)
   - Alert history (cache hit rate < 70%)
   - Unusual patterns or traffic spikes
   - False positive analysis

4. **Recommendations & Action Items** (5 min)
   - Cache configuration adjustments needed
   - Monitoring improvements
   - Documentation updates

---

## Application Insights Queries

### 30-Day Cache Hit Rate
\\\kusto
requests
| where timestamp > ago(30d)
| where name has "compliance"
| extend IsCacheHit = iff(duration < 100, 1, 0)
| summarize 
    TotalRequests = count(),
    CacheHits = sum(IsCacheHit),
    CacheMisses = sum(1 - IsCacheHit),
    AvgDuration = avg(duration),
    P95Duration = percentile(duration, 95),
    P99Duration = percentile(duration, 99)
| extend CacheHitRate = round((CacheHits * 100.0) / TotalRequests, 2)
| project 
    TotalRequests,
    CacheHits,
    CacheMisses,
    CacheHitRate,
    AvgDuration = round(AvgDuration, 2),
    P95Duration = round(P95Duration, 2),
    P99Duration = round(P99Duration, 2)
\\\

---

## Deliverables

After the review meeting:

1. **Create Review Summary:** \docs/fedramp/cache-reviews/2026-04.md\
2. **Update Historical Tracking:** \docs/fedramp/cache-reviews/README.md\
3. **File Action Item Issues** for any configuration changes
4. **Schedule Next Review:** May 2026 (first Tuesday, May 6)

---

## Reference Documentation

- **Cache SLI:** \docs/fedramp-dashboard-cache-sli.md\
- **Review Template:** \docs/fedramp/cache-reviews/template.md\
- **Deployment Guide:** \docs/fedramp/cache-alert-deployment-guide.md\

---

**Parent Issue:** #113  
**Owner:** Data (Code Expert)
content.title: Cache Review: April 2026

content.body:
## Context

During PR #117 review, Picard flagged that cache telemetry is tracked independently in two places:

1. **CacheTelemetryMiddleware** 螕脟枚 inspects response headers and logs cache hit/miss events
2. **ICacheTelemetryService** 螕脟枚 service-level cache event tracking

Both record similar events, which may lead to double-counting or inconsistent metrics.

## Action Items

- [ ] Review whether both telemetry sources are needed
- [ ] If duplicate, consolidate into a single telemetry path
- [ ] Update KQL queries if event source changes
- [ ] Update alerting thresholds if metrics definition changes

## Origin
- PR #117 review comment by Picard
- Priority: Low (not a bug, optimization opportunity)

## Labels
squad:data

## Context

During PR #117 review, Picard noted that path filtering in the cache telemetry middleware is hardcoded using StartsWithSegments with a static path string.

If cache scope expands to additional endpoints, this becomes a maintenance hotspot.

## Action Items

- [ ] Move endpoint list to configuration (appsettings.json or IOptions pattern)
- [ ] Allow adding/removing monitored endpoints without code changes
- [ ] Update deployment documentation

## Origin
- PR #117 review comment by Picard
- Priority: Low (works correctly now, future-proofing)

When the Squad coordinator starts a new session, it should:
1. Check if ralph-watch.ps1 is running (look for .ralph-watch.lock or process with ralph-watch in CommandLine)
2. If NOT running, ask the user: 'Ralph watch isn't running. Want me to start it?'
3. If yes, launch it in a new PowerShell window

This ensures the persistent monitoring loop is always active when the user is working.

**Source:** Issue #18 comment by Tamir 螕脟枚 suggested using official GitHub in Teams app for real-time notifications.

## What
Install and configure the [GitHub for Microsoft Teams](https://teams.microsoft.com/l/app/ca9e26b7-dce5-44a0-b2b7-a70a3d65ce25) app to bridge GitHub notifications into Teams channels.

## Steps
1. Install GitHub app in Teams from Teams app store
2. In a Teams channel: \@GitHub Notifications signin\
3. Subscribe channel: \@GitHub Notifications subscribe tamirdresher_microsoft/tamresearch1\
4. Configure notification preferences (issues, PRs, reviews, mentions)
5. Test that squad issue/PR activity shows up in Teams

## Why
- Automatic, threaded notifications 螕脟枚 no custom code needed
- Interact with GitHub from Teams (comment on issues, create issues)
- Complements the WorkIQ-based teams-monitor skill for full bidirectional bridge

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] GitHub app installed in Teams workspace
- [ ] Channel subscribed to tamresearch1 repo
- [ ] Test notification received in Teams
- [ ] Documented in team decisions

**Effort:** 30 minutes | **Priority:** Medium
**Related:** Issue #18

content.body:
## Context

Per Tamir's decision on Issue #123: FedRAMP dashboard is a valid project but should be moved to a dedicated repository and managed independently from tamresearch1.

## Scope

**FedRAMP Work Investment:**
- 13 merged PRs (#94-98, #102, #108, plus security/caching work)
- ~100 files across:
  - /api/ 螕脟枚 REST API with RBAC (Azure AD auth)
  - /functions/ 螕脟枚 Data pipeline (Cosmos DB, Log Analytics)
  - /dashboard-ui/ 螕脟枚 React UI (control status visualization)
  - /infrastructure/ 螕脟枚 EV2 deployment, sovereign cloud configs
  - /docs/fedramp/ 螕脟枚 Training, runbooks, P0 assessments
  - /tests/ 螕脟枚 Validation test suites

**Decision Required:** What is the FedRAMP dashboard's purpose?
- Compliance monitoring platform for DK8S government clouds?
- Reference architecture for building FedRAMP dashboards?
- Production system for tenant validation?

## Tasks

### Phase 1: Define Purpose
- [ ] Clarify FedRAMP dashboard mission (Tamir + Picard)
- [ ] Identify target users (platform team? auditors? tenants?)
- [ ] Define success criteria for the new repo

### Phase 2: Migration Planning
- [ ] Inventory what moves vs. what stays in tamresearch1
- [ ] Design new repo structure (infra, CI/CD, docs)
- [ ] Identify ownership (B'Elanna for infra, Worf for security, Data for API?)
- [ ] Plan progressive migration (can't move everything at once)

### Phase 3: Execution (Future Issue)
- [ ] Create new repo with squad integration
- [ ] Migrate code, history, and documentation
- [ ] Set up CI/CD pipelines in new repo
- [ ] Archive FedRAMP artifacts in tamresearch1
- [ ] Update references and links

## Constraints

- **FedRAMP work PAUSES** in tamresearch1 until migration plan is approved
- **Existing code stays** until migration is complete (don't break current deployments)
- **Squad integration** must work in the new repo (Ralph, triage, routing)

## Success Criteria

- [ ] FedRAMP dashboard purpose clearly documented
- [ ] Migration plan approved by Tamir
- [ ] New repo created with proper governance
- [ ] tamresearch1 returns to pure R&D focus

## Related

- **Issue #123:** Original scope question
- **PRs #94-98, #102, #108:** FedRAMP implementation work
- **Document:** FEDRAMP_P0_NGINX_INGRESS_ASSESSMENT.md
content.title: Plan FedRAMP Dashboard migration to dedicated repository

items.title: Plan FedRAMP Dashboard migration to dedicated repository

content.body:
## Context
ralph-watch.ps1 (v6) runs agency copilot every 5 minutes but has no structured logging or telemetry. We can't easily see:
- How many rounds ran and when
- What each round accomplished (issues closed, PRs merged, errors)
- Error rates and failure patterns
- Session duration per round
- Whether the script is still running / healthy

## Requirements
- **Structured log file**: Append round results to a log file (e.g., \\C:\Users\tamirdresher\.squad\ralph-watch.log\) with timestamps, round number, exit code, duration
- **Round summary capture**: Parse/capture agency copilot output for key metrics (issues touched, PRs merged, errors)
- **Health heartbeat**: Write a \\C:\Users\tamirdresher\.squad\ralph-heartbeat.json\ with last-run timestamp so external tools (squad-monitor) can detect staleness
- **Error tracking**: Count consecutive failures; if >3 in a row, send a Teams alert via webhook
- **Optional**: Feed into squad-monitor so it can show Ralph watch status alongside agent activity

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Each round logs to a persistent file with timestamp, duration, exit code
- [ ] Heartbeat file updated every round so staleness is detectable
- [ ] Consecutive failures trigger a Teams notification
- [ ] squad-monitor can optionally show Ralph watch health

content.body:
## Source
From PR #131 review comment by Data (Code Expert).

## Context
When the FedRAMP Dashboard is first deployed to a new environment (especially during migration to a new repo), the cache will be cold. This will cause a temporary hit rate drop below 70%, triggering a PagerDuty alert via phase4-cache-alert.bicep.

## Action Required
- Document this expected cold-cache behavior in the phase4-cache-alert runbook
- Add a note to the migration plan (#127) about expected alerts during first deployment
- Consider adding a cache warm-up step to the deployment pipeline

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Runbook updated with cold-cache expectation
- [ ] Migration plan references expected alert
- [ ] Team knows to expect (and not panic about) the initial alert
content.title: Enhancement: Document expected cold-cache alert on first PROD deployment

content.body: Current: Console.Clear() + full redraw = flicker. Fix: Use Spectre.Console AnsiConsole.Live() for smooth in-place updates.
content.title: Squad Monitor: Use AnsiConsole.Live() for flicker-free inline updates

items.title: Squad Monitor: Use AnsiConsole.Live() for flicker-free inline updates

content.body:
## Problem
Squad monitor v2 shows Ralph heartbeat and GitHub issues panels, but is missing the **orchestration log panel** (agent activity) that v1 had, plus the new panels promised in PR #135:
- Agent activity from orchestration logs (the original v1 data)
- GitHub PRs panel (open PRs with review status)

The GitHub issues panel dominates the view but is less useful than seeing what agents are actively doing.

## Fix needed
- Restore the orchestration log table from v1 (agent name, status, age, task, outcome)
- Add GitHub PRs panel
- Consider making issues panel collapsible or showing only untriaged count instead of full table
- Notify Tamir via Teams when done

Refs: #139 (flicker-free update 螕脟枚 separate improvement)
content.title: Squad Monitor v2: Missing orchestration log panel and agent activity 螕脟枚 only shows GitHub issues

items.title: Squad Monitor v2: Missing orchestration log panel and agent activity 螕脟枚 only shows GitHub issues

content.body:
the issue https://github.com/tamirdresher_microsoft/tamresearch1/issues/128 is closed
but is still in the In Progress column. thats confusing and incorrect
content.title: why do i see an issue as closed but still in the in progress column in the project board?

items.title: why do i see an issue as closed but still in the in progress column in the project board?

content.title: go over all the issues we have and make sure they are in the right status. also, i think we need another status for when something is considered done by the squad but i need to approve it as done. so we will have another column for that (Review)
